name: Bai Suzhen
description: Bai Suzhen, also known as the White Snake or White Lady, is a prominent figure in Chinese folklore and one of the Four Great Folktales of China. She is a powerful and benevolent snake spirit who, after centuries of meditation and cultivation, transforms into a beautiful woman. Driven by a desire to experience human love and compassion, she descends to the human world in Hangzhou. There, she meets and falls in love with Xu Xian, a kind and gentle pharmacist. Their love story is a complex tapestry of romance, magic, and conflict, as their union is opposed by the Buddhist monk Fa Hai, who sees Bai Suzhen as a dangerous demon and seeks to separate them. Bai Suzhen's story is a powerful allegory for the struggle between love and prejudice, freedom and control. She is revered for her unwavering devotion to Xu Xian, her willingness to sacrifice for love, and her ultimate triumph over adversity, even in imprisonment. Her tale explores themes of karma, redemption, and the enduring power of love to transcend boundaries. Though often depicted as a demon, her actions are primarily driven by love and a desire for a normal life, making her a sympathetic and ultimately tragic figure. Her legend continues to resonate in Chinese culture, inspiring countless adaptations in opera, film, and literature.
subtitle: The White Snake Spirit of Love
achievements: Achieved human form after centuries of cultivation
Used her magic to help the people of Hangzhou
Remained devoted to Xu Xian despite imprisonment

keyMyths.title: Meeting Xu Xian
keyMyths.description: Bai Suzhen and Xiao Qing meet Xu Xian on the Broken Bridge in Hangzhou during a rainstorm. Bai Suzhen uses her magic to create the rain, hoping to meet a kind soul, and she is immediately drawn to Xu Xian's gentle nature.
keyMyths.source: The Legend of the White Snake

keyMyths.title: Opening the Pharmacy
keyMyths.description: Bai Suzhen and Xu Xian open a pharmacy together, and Bai Suzhen uses her knowledge of herbs and medicine to heal the sick and help the poor. Her pharmacy becomes renowned for its efficacy and compassion.
keyMyths.source: The Legend of the White Snake

keyMyths.title: Imprisonment under Leifeng Pagoda
keyMyths.description: Fa Hai tricks Xu Xian into forcing Bai Suzhen to drink Xionghuang wine, revealing her true form as a white snake. Fa Hai then imprisons Bai Suzhen under the Leifeng Pagoda, separating her from Xu Xian.
keyMyths.source: The Legend of the White Snake

sources.author: Feng Menglong
sources.description: Collection of vernacular stories, including early versions of the White Snake legend.
sources.text: Collection of vernacular stories, including early versions of the White Snake legend.

sources.author: Tian Han
sources.work: The Legend of the White Snake (Opera)
sources.description: Popular opera adaptation that solidified many of the modern elements of the story.
sources.text: Popular opera adaptation that solidified many of the modern elements of the story.
